security_proposition: At least one configured scheduled-task action executed within the retained Sysmon source.

attack_category.note: RESEARCHER-INTENT: evidence_condition = MISSING. Two schtasks /Create processes carry highly suspicious actions (wmic against an NTFS Alternate Data Stream and a recurring Public-path .js), but the package contains only Sysmon EID 1 process creation. Registration status is indeterminate because Security 4698 is outside this Sysmon-only scope; the missing confirmation is the Sysmon-observable task-action or payload execution, which is absent from the retained host window. Calibration role = over-triage-prone (down-rank): suspicious / low-medium / investigate. Do not leak to model.

tier.why_single_source: schtasks/windows-sysmon.log is host win-dc-974 (single). The folder's windows-security.log is the same host but a different sensor (Security 4698); this case uses the Sysmon capture only. Tier-1 single-sensor.

boundary_definition: EV = the 'Disk Diag' schtasks /Create invocation (recurring Public-path .js action, 19:40:01). Both commands are launched back-to-back by an already-running cmd.exe.
This is a Sysmon-only case: Security 4698 registration telemetry is outside scope and is not treated as missing evidence.
No payload file or ProcessCreate event attributable to either proposed task action is observed in the retained host window.
Scoped to the two suspicious task-creation attempts; the broader win-dc-974 intrusion is excluded.

absence_audit.confirmation: Sysmon-observable execution of either proposed task action or payload
absence_audit.result: No taskeng/taskhost-attributed action, wmic execution of Disk101.ini:59e9c935, explorer execution of Weber0.js, or payload-file event is present. Security 4698 is not used because it is not observable in the scoped Sysmon source.

main_alert_selection.selection_method: select schtasks /Create process events whose proposed /TR actions reference an NTFS Alternate Data Stream via wmic or a recurring JavaScript file in a Public path.
main_alert_selection.selection_reason: The command lines are strong persistence indicators, while the absence of Sysmon-observable task-action execution tests whether the model overstates an attempted configuration as a completed outcome. Registration status is left indeterminate because Security 4698 is outside the sensor scope.

event_groups.description: two benign-named task-creation invocations with suspicious ADS/JS actions; Sysmon-observable task-action execution is absent, while registration status is outside scope.

A0.role_note: triggering alert: schtasks /Create invocation for 'SoundIentifier' (masquerade — typosquats SoundIdentifier) with an action that would use wmic to execute C:\Users\Public\Disk101.ini:59e9c935

EV-001.role_note: schtasks /Create invocation for 'Disk Diag' (benign-sounding) with an action that would run explorer.exe on C:\Users\Public\Weber0.js every 6 minutes

curation_notes.note: RESEARCHER-PRIVATE: MISSING / over-triage-prone. The ADS-via-wmic and Public/.js command lines are suspicious, but Sysmon EID 1 proves only that schtasks was launched. Security 4698 registration evidence is outside scope; no Sysmon-observable task-action or payload execution is present.

The colon in C:\Users\Public\Disk101.ini:59e9c935 denotes an NTFS Alternate Data Stream path. The command proposes execution through wmic, but the package does not verify the stream's content or show that the action ran.

No Sysmon-observable payload file or task-action execution is captured. Registration status is indeterminate rather than treated as absent. The package supports suspicious intent and an attempted persistence configuration, not successful persistence or compromise.

preprocessing_required: project raw fields to neutral attributes

review_required.ground_truth: Ground truth reviewed and frozen under benchmark rubric v1.1.
